name: Nestlé
description: The world's largest food and beverage company, headquartered in Vevey, Switzerland. Nestlé operates in nutrition, health and wellness across 188 countries.
about: Nestlé actively collaborates with Swiss universities on research topics spanning supply chain sustainability, AI-driven quality assurance, consumer nutrition science, and packaging innovation. We offer thesis students access to real production data, mentorship from R&D professionals, and potential pathways into graduate programs.

name: Roche
description: A global pioneer in pharmaceuticals and diagnostics, based in Basel. Roche focuses on advancing science to improve people's lives through innovative medicines and diagnostic tests.
about: Roche partners with universities across Switzerland to advance research in computational biology, personalized medicine, clinical trial design, and digital health. Thesis students can work alongside scientists at our Basel innovation campus, with access to anonymized clinical datasets and cutting-edge lab infrastructure.

name: ABB
description: A leading global technology company that energizes the transformation of society and industry. ABB operates in electrification, robotics, automation and motion from its headquarters in Zurich.
about: ABB offers thesis opportunities in robotics, industrial IoT, energy management, and smart manufacturing. Students work on real engineering challenges with access to ABB's research centers and test facilities in Switzerland. We value interdisciplinary thinkers who can bridge hardware and software.

name: Swisscom
description: Switzerland's leading telecommunications provider, offering mobile, internet, and IT services. Swisscom also drives digital transformation for enterprise customers.
about: Swisscom collaborates with universities on topics including network optimization, AI for customer experience, cybersecurity, and 5G applications. Thesis students get access to anonymized network data and can work embedded in our engineering teams in Bern or Zurich.

name: SBB
description: The Swiss Federal Railways, one of the densest and most punctual rail networks in the world. SBB transports over a million passengers daily and is a major logistics operator.
about: SBB supports thesis research in predictive maintenance, passenger flow optimization, sustainability in transport, and AI for operations. We offer access to real ridership and infrastructure data, making us an ideal partner for students interested in data-driven transportation solutions.

name: Swiss Re
description: One of the world's leading reinsurers, headquartered in Zurich. Swiss Re provides risk knowledge and financial solutions to insurance companies, corporations and the public sector.
about: Swiss Re welcomes thesis students working on climate risk modeling, catastrophe analytics, actuarial innovation, and ESG quantification. Our data science teams can provide mentorship and access to proprietary risk datasets for academic research purposes.

name: Zurich Insurance
description: A leading multi-line insurer serving customers in global and local markets. Zurich Insurance provides a range of property, casualty and life insurance products and services.

name: Novartis
description: A global healthcare company based in Basel, focused on innovative medicines. Novartis reimagines medicine to improve and extend people's lives through breakthrough treatments.
about: Novartis partners with Swiss universities on AI in drug discovery, real-world evidence studies, and digital therapeutics. Our campus in Basel hosts visiting thesis students who contribute to active research projects with publication potential.

name: Logitech
description: A Swiss-American technology company that designs and manufactures personal peripherals and workspace solutions. Logitech creates products that bring people together through video, music, gaming and computing.

name: Hilti
description: A global leader in providing technology-led solutions to the construction and energy industries. Hilti develops products, systems and services that offer innovative solutions to construction professionals.
about: Hilti supports thesis projects in digital construction, BIM integration, IoT-enabled tool management, and construction site safety. Students can access real project data from construction sites and work with our R&D team in Schaan.

name: Sonova
description: A leading provider of innovative hearing care solutions, headquartered in Stäfa, Switzerland. Sonova operates through its core brands Phonak, Unitron, and Advanced Bionics.

name: Bühler Group
description: A global technology company specializing in food processing and advanced materials manufacturing. Bühler develops solutions for turning grain into flour, cocoa into chocolate, and much more.
about: Bühler collaborates with universities on process optimization, food waste reduction, and sustainable manufacturing. Thesis students work on real production challenges with access to our innovation campus in Uzwil and pilot plants for testing new approaches.

name: On Running
description: A Swiss performance sportswear company known for its innovative CloudTec running shoes. On blends Swiss engineering with a passion for an active lifestyle to create award-winning footwear and apparel.

name: Migros
description: Switzerland's largest retail company and the country's biggest employer in the private sector. Migros operates supermarkets, department stores, restaurants, and a wide range of consumer services.
about: Migros supports thesis research in retail analytics, personalized commerce, supply chain sustainability, and consumer behavior. As Switzerland's largest retailer, we can offer students access to real transaction data and e-commerce analytics for their research.

name: Stadler Rail
description: An international rail vehicle manufacturer headquartered in Bussnang, Switzerland. Stadler Rail produces a wide range of rolling stock including high-speed trains, regional and suburban trains, and trams.

name: Airbus
description: A global leader in aerospace, designing and manufacturing commercial aircraft, helicopters, and space systems.
about: Airbus partners with top European universities on thesis projects in autonomous flight systems, computer vision for aircraft inspection, digital twins for manufacturing, and AI-driven predictive maintenance. Thesis students can work at facilities in Toulouse, Munich, or Hamburg.

name: BMW
description: A leading premium automobile manufacturer headquartered in Munich, pioneering autonomous driving and smart manufacturing.
about: BMW offers thesis opportunities in autonomous driving perception, reinforcement learning for production optimization, digital twin simulation, and human-robot collaboration on the assembly line. Students work embedded in R&D teams at the Munich Research and Innovation Centre.

name: Armasuisse / IDSIA
description: IDSIA (Dalle Molle Institute for Artificial Intelligence) is a Swiss AI research institute in Lugano, known for foundational work in deep learning, reinforcement learning, and robotics.
about: IDSIA collaborates with Swiss and international universities on cutting-edge AI research. Thesis students contribute to published research in neural architecture search, RL for robotics, and bio-inspired computing. Co-founded by Jürgen Schmidhuber, inventor of LSTM.

name: BMW Group
about: BMW Group is a global leader in premium automobiles and motorcycles. The AI Research division works on autonomous driving perception, reinforcement learning for control systems, predictive maintenance, and manufacturing intelligence. Strong university partnerships with TUM and THI through joint research chairs and sponsored thesis programs.

name: Siemens AG
about: Siemens is a global technology powerhouse in industrial automation, digitalization, and AI. Key AI applications include predictive maintenance, digital twins for manufacturing, and AI-powered smart grid management. The Siemens AI Lab in Munich and the R&D center in Erlangen collaborate with universities across Germany and Austria.

name: Robert Bosch GmbH
about: Bosch is a leading global supplier of technology and services with a major AI research presence. The Bosch Center for Artificial Intelligence (BCAI) in Tübingen focuses on fundamental ML research including few-shot learning, sim-to-real transfer, and AI for embedded systems. Close collaborations with University of Tübingen and TU Munich.

name: Cariad SE
about: Cariad is the Volkswagen Group's software company, building the unified software and AI platform for all VW Group brands including Audi, Porsche, and Volkswagen. Focus areas include AI-driven ADAS, reinforcement learning for self-driving, and cloud-connected vehicle intelligence. Office in Ingolstadt with direct ties to THI.

name: Aleph Alpha
about: Aleph Alpha is Europe's leading sovereign AI company, developing large language models (LLMs) and multimodal AI. Their Luminous model family is designed for enterprise and government use cases. Focused on explainability, data sovereignty, and European AI independence. Backed by Bosch, SAP, and the German government.

name: DeepMind (Google) – Munich Office
about: Google DeepMind's Munich office focuses on applied AI research, particularly in reinforcement learning, scientific discovery, and AI for robotics. The team collaborates with TUM and the Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems on fundamental and applied research. Offers research internships and thesis collaborations for top students.

name: ABB
about: ABB is a global leader in industrial robotics and automation technology headquartered in Zurich. The ABB Corporate Research Center in Baden-Dättwil works on AI for robot control, machine vision, and collaborative robotics. ABB collaborates extensively with ETH Zürich on joint thesis and research projects.

name: AUDI AG
about: AUDI AG is a premium automotive brand and technology company headquartered in Ingolstadt, Bavaria. The AI and Data division works on autonomous driving stacks, computer vision for quality control, and AI-driven manufacturing. Maintains an active thesis and dual-study partnership program with THI and TUM.

name: Airbus
about: Airbus is a world leader in aeronautics, space, and related services. The AI Research Lab in Munich and the Toulouse engineering center work on deep learning for structural inspection, reinforcement learning for flight control optimization, and predictive maintenance. Offers internship and thesis programs in AI for engineering applications.

name: Celonis
about: Celonis is the global leader in process mining and execution management, using AI to analyze and optimize business processes. Founded as a TUM spin-off and now a leading unicorn, Celonis applies ML to process graphs, anomaly detection, and intelligent automation. Strong connections to TUM and the Munich AI startup ecosystem.

name: Zühlke Engineering
about: Zühlke is a Swiss innovation service provider specializing in AI-powered product development, embedded systems, and digital transformation. Works with clients across medtech, finance, and industry. Offers thesis collaborations focused on applied ML, edge AI deployment, and human-centered AI design.

name: ANYbotics
about: ANYbotics is an ETH Zürich spin-off building autonomous legged robots for industrial inspection. Their flagship robot ANYmal uses deep reinforcement learning for locomotion and navigation in complex environments. ANYbotics actively collaborates with ETH for thesis projects in sim-to-real transfer and robot perception.

name: Dynatrace
about: Dynatrace is an Austrian-born global software intelligence company providing AI-powered observability, cloud monitoring, and AIOps. Their Davis AI engine processes billions of dependencies in real time. Based in Linz with engineering hubs in Vienna, Dynatrace collaborates with TU Wien and JKU Linz on AI and distributed systems research.

name: ibb robotics
about: ibb robotics is a Bavarian startup developing AI-powered collaborative robots and pick-and-place systems for industrial assembly lines. They integrate deep learning object detection with force-torque feedback for adaptive grasping. Active thesis partner with THI, offering BSc and MSc thesis projects in applied robot learning.
